project_structure: Cocos Creator editor extension (ccc-smart-component-manager v1.2.1) with flat module layout: panel/index.js contains all UI (CSS+HTML+JS in Editor.Panel.extend), i18n/zh.js for Chinese translations, i18n-helper.js for locale management, data-manager.js for state, search-engine.js for component search. The panel runs inside Cocos Creator's Electron-based editor shell, rendering as a dockable panel via Shadow DOM (:host selector).

relevant_files.rationale: Contains ALL CSS styles (lines 60-1300), HTML template (lines 1302-1344), and web component usage (<ui-button> in 20+ locations). This is the sole file defining layout, styling, and UI component dependencies. Every CSS feature in use is defined here.

Contains all 90+ i18n keys organized in sections: MAIN_MENU, PANEL, ADVANCED_PANEL, SEARCH, SETTINGS, ARIA, COMMON. New landscape layout will need new keys for compact mode labels, layout toggle, and any new UI sections.
Manages i18n translation loading and the t() function with {{param}} template interpolation. Supports zh/en with zh fallback. Understanding this is needed to know how to add new i18n keys correctly.
Defines panel dimensions (width:450, height:120, min-height:60) and panel type 'dockable'. These constraints directly affect landscape layout feasibility and minimum size requirements.
Auto-docks the panel next to the Inspector using Cocos Creator internal APIs (dockUtil.dropTab). Confirms the panel runs inside ui-dock-panel which constrains available viewport dimensions.

patterns: CSS Architecture: Uses CSS custom properties extensively (30+ variables in :host). Layout uses flexbox (display:flex) as primary layout mechanism with CSS Grid (display:grid, grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax())) for the search results list. All spacing uses var(--spacing-*) tokens. Responsive design uses @media queries for height breakpoints (600px, 800px) and width (600px). Animation uses @keyframes with cubic-bezier easing. The :host selector confirms Shadow DOM encapsulation. Heavy use of !important on ui-button styles suggests fighting Shadow DOM style encapsulation of Cocos Creator web components.
dependencies:
CSS Feature Compatibility for Electron 2-4 (Chromium 58-69):

SAFE (fully supported):
- CSS Custom Properties (var(--*)) - Chromium 49+
- Flexbox (display:flex, flex-direction, flex-wrap, justify-content, align-items, flex-grow/shrink/basis) - Chromium 29+
- CSS Grid (display:grid, grid-template-columns, grid-template-rows, repeat(), minmax()) - Chromium 57+
- gap (in Grid context) - Chromium 66+ (Electron 3+)
- calc() - Chromium 26+
- transform, transition, animation, @keyframes - Chromium 36+
- box-shadow, border-radius - fully supported
- linear-gradient() - Chromium 26+
- ::placeholder pseudo-element - Chromium 57+
- :focus-visible - NOT supported (Chromium 86+), currently behind @media query so safe
- overflow: auto/hidden/scroll - fully supported
- scrollbar-width: thin - NOT supported (Firefox only), harmless fallback
- scroll-snap-type - Chromium 66+ (Electron 3+)
- accent-color - NOT supported (Chromium 93+), graceful degradation
- @media (prefers-color-scheme) - NOT supported (Chromium 76+), harmless
- @media (prefers-contrast) - NOT supported (Chromium 96+), harmless
- @media (prefers-reduced-motion) - Chromium 74+ (Electron 4+ only)

RISKY for Electron 2 (Chromium 58):
- gap in flexbox context - NOT supported until Chromium 84. Currently used in .container, .row, #coms. MUST use margin fallback or only use gap in grid context.
- scroll-snap-type: x proximity - Chromium 66+ only (Electron 3+)

CRITICAL ISSUE: 'gap' property on flex containers is used throughout (lines 134, 141, 218) but only works in CSS Grid on Chromium 58-83. For Electron 2 compatibility, flex gap MUST be replaced with margin-based spacing.

i18n Dependencies:
- 90+ translation keys in i18n/zh.js across 7 sections
- Only zh.js exists; en.js falls back to zh
- Template interpolation uses {{paramName}} syntax
- Keys are referenced via t('DOTTED.KEY.PATH') helper
- New landscape layout will likely need: compact mode labels, layout direction indicators, overflow/truncation text

Cocos Creator Web Components:
- <ui-button>: Used as primary interactive element (20+ instances). Rendered as Shadow DOM custom element. Styling requires !important overrides. Supports standard click events. No built-in icon slot - icons inserted as innerHTML (SVG).
- Native <input>, <select>, <input type='checkbox'> used for form controls (NOT Cocos ui-input/ui-select)
- No <ui-input>, <ui-select>, <ui-checkbox> used - all form elements are native HTML
- ui-dock-panel: Container element provided by Cocos Creator editor shell
integration_points:
1. Cocos Creator Editor.Panel.extend() API: Panel lifecycle (ready/close), style/template as string properties. All CSS must be inline in the style string.
2. <ui-button> web component: Shadow DOM boundary means styles need !important to penetrate. Standard DOM events (click, mousedown) work normally. Cannot easily make icon-only compact buttons without overriding internal padding.
3. Panel dimensions: package.json defines initial width:450, height:120, min-height:60. These affect landscape layout - the panel can be resized by the user but starts narrow and short.
4. Docking system: Panel docks next to Inspector via template-auto-dock.js. In landscape mode, available width depends on editor layout.
5. i18n integration: t() function from i18n-helper.js used in template literals during panel construction. Adding new keys requires updating i18n/zh.js only (en falls back to zh).
constraints:
1. FLEX GAP INCOMPATIBILITY: gap property on flex containers does NOT work in Electron 2 (Chromium 58). Must use margin-based spacing or restrict gap to CSS Grid containers only. This affects .container, .row, #coms, and many other flex layouts.
2. UI-BUTTON STYLING: <ui-button> has Shadow DOM encapsulation requiring !important overrides. Making compact icon-only buttons requires careful size overrides (min-width, min-height, padding). Internal button structure cannot be modified.
3. SHADOW DOM SCOPE: All styles are scoped to :host. No global CSS leakage but also no access to parent editor styles.
4. SINGLE FILE UI: All CSS+HTML+JS in panel/index.js. No CSS preprocessor, no separate stylesheet files. Large style strings must remain manageable.
5. NO CSS GRID GAP IN ELECTRON 2: While CSS Grid itself works (Chromium 57+), the gap shorthand in grid context works from Chromium 66+ (Electron 3). For Electron 2 strict compat, use grid-gap (old syntax, Chromium 57+).
6. SCROLLBAR STYLING: scrollbar-width:thin is Firefox-only and has no effect. ::-webkit-scrollbar works in all Electron versions.
7. MEDIA QUERY LIMITATIONS: prefers-color-scheme, prefers-contrast, prefers-reduced-motion are Electron 4+ features. They gracefully degrade (ignored) in older versions.
8. I18N CHINESE-ONLY: Only zh.js translation file exists. English falls back to Chinese text. Any new UI text needs zh.js keys at minimum.

clarification_needs.question: What is the minimum Electron version that must be supported?
clarification_needs.context: Flexbox gap (used in 10+ places) only works from Chromium 84 (Electron 10). CSS Grid gap works from Chromium 66 (Electron 3). The old grid-gap syntax works from Chromium 57 (Electron 2). The refactor approach differs significantly: Electron 2 requires margin-based flex spacing everywhere, while Electron 3+ allows grid gap, and Electron 10+ allows flex gap.
options: Electron 2+ (Chromium 58) - maximum compatibility, must replace all flex gap with margins
Electron 3+ (Chromium 66) - grid gap works, flex gap still needs margins
Electron 4+ (Chromium 69) - same as 3 but media queries work
Modern only (Electron 10+/Chromium 84+) - all current CSS features work

clarification_needs.question: Should the landscape layout use CSS Grid or Flexbox as primary layout mechanism?
clarification_needs.context: Current code uses Flexbox for main layout (container, rows) and CSS Grid only for search results list. A landscape two-column layout could use either: Grid for the overall panel layout (cleaner column definitions) or Flexbox (more compatible but requires more wrapper elements). Grid is supported in Electron 2+ but gap behavior differs.
options: CSS Grid for panel layout + Flexbox for inner components (cleanest approach, Electron 2+)
Flexbox only (maximum compatibility, more wrapper divs needed)
Keep current approach - Flexbox primary with Grid only for result lists

clarification_needs.question: Are new English translations needed for the landscape UI, or is Chinese-only acceptable?
clarification_needs.context: Currently only i18n/zh.js exists. The i18n-helper.js attempts to load en.js but falls back to zh.js. A UI refactor adding new layout features may warrant proper English translations, especially for an open-source Cocos Creator package.
options: Add en.js with English translations for all new and existing keys

_metadata.task_description: Major UI refactor for compact landscape panel. Need to understand: (1) What CSS features work in Electron 2-4 (flexbox, grid, gap, custom properties, etc.), (2) Current i18n key usage and what new keys might be needed, (3) Cocos Creator ui-button/ui-input web component constraints, (4) Any other dependencies that affect UI layout decisions.
